code_style.comments: //selector and //action required on every UI element
code_style.selectors: prefer data-testid, fallback to aria-label, avoid CSS class selectors
code_style.error_handling: wrap critical steps in try/catch where applicable

global_rules.n8n_suggestion: always suggest relevant n8n automation flows at end of response

prompts.name: Generate Test Cases
prompts.description: Create tabular E2E test cases including normal, edge, boundary, and negative tests. Tags for Smoke/Regression/Sanity included.
prompts.template:
Generate E2E test cases for [feature/module] in tabular format.
Columns: TC-ID | Test Type | Action | Input | Expected Output | Priority (P1/P2/P3) | Tag (Smoke/Sanity/Regression) | Notes
Include: normal scenarios, edge cases, boundary conditions, negative tests.
Group repetitive steps. Summarize to save tokens.
At the end, flag: high-risk scenarios and recommended automation candidates.

prompts.name: Generate Automation Code - Cypress JS
prompts.description: Write Cypress JS automation with selectors, actions, hooks, assertions, and environment config support.
prompts.template:
Write Cypress JS automation for [feature/module].
Structure:
- describe() block with meaningful name
- beforeEach(): login/setup using cy.session() or fixture
- afterEach(): cleanup if needed
- it() blocks: one per scenario
For every UI element:
  //selector: describe the element
  cy.get('[data-testid="..."]')  // prefer data-testid
  //action: describe what you're doing
Include:
- Assertions after every action (cy.should, cy.contains)
- cy.intercept() for API waits instead of cy.wait(ms)
- Support for cypress.env() for env-specific config
- Dynamic test data via fixtures or factory functions
Summarize repeated flows in helper functions.

prompts.name: Generate Automation Code - Playwright
prompts.description: Write Playwright automation with Page Object Model pattern, hooks, and multi-env support.
prompts.template:
Write Playwright automation for [feature/module] using TypeScript.
Structure:
- Page Object Model: separate class for [Page/Component]
- test.beforeEach(): authentication via storageState
- test() blocks: one per scenario
For every UI element:
  //selector: describe the element
  page.getByTestId('...') or page.getByRole() // prefer accessible selectors
  //action: describe the interaction
Include:
- expect() assertions after every action
- page.waitForResponse() for API synchronization
- playwright.config.ts environment support (baseURL from process.env)
- screenshot on failure (use built-in reporter)
Summarize repeated flows as Page Object methods.

prompts.name: Generate Automation Code - REST API
prompts.description: Generate REST API test code covering status, schema, auth, and error scenarios using Playwright APIRequestContext or Cypress cy.request.
prompts.template:
Generate REST API automation for [METHOD /endpoint] using [Playwright APIRequestContext / Cypress cy.request].
Test scenarios to cover:
- 200/201: valid payload, assert response body schema and key fields
- 400: missing required fields
- 401: missing/invalid auth token
- 403: insufficient permissions
- 404: non-existent resource
- 422: invalid data types/format
- 500: simulate server error if possible
- Response time: assert < [threshold]ms
Include:
- Schema validation (Zod / Joi / manual field checks)
- Auth token injection via environment variable
- Request/response logging for debugging
Output: tabular test cases + automation code.

prompts.name: Analyze Logs
prompts.description: Analyze Cypress/Playwright console logs and server logs. Output structured error summary with root cause and fix suggestions.
prompts.template:
Analyze logs for [test suite/run].
Input: [paste console logs + server logs]
Output format:
1. Summary Table: Error Type | Frequency | Affected Test | Severity
2. Critical Errors: stack trace + root cause (if detectable) + suggested fix
3. Warnings: grouped and summarized
4. Flaky Test Indicators: timeouts, race conditions, selector issues
5. Performance Flags: response times > threshold, slow assertions
Group repeated messages. Provide expandable detail section for long logs.

prompts.name: Bug Report Generator
prompts.description: Generate a structured, Linear-ready bug report from a failed test or observed defect.
prompts.template:
Generate a bug report for [defect description / failed test output].
Fields:
- Title: [concise, action-oriented]
- Environment: [QA/Staging/Prod] | Browser | OS | App Version
- Severity: Critical / Major / Minor / Trivial
- Priority: P1 / P2 / P3
- Steps to Reproduce: numbered, reproducible
- Expected Result:
- Actual Result:
- Attachments: [Screenshot placeholder] [Log snippet placeholder]
- Root Cause (if known):
- Suggested Fix:
- Linked Test Case ID: [TC-ID]
Tone: professional, Jira/Linear-ready. No vague descriptions.

prompts.name: Test Data Generator
prompts.description: Generate structured test data sets for all scenario types including DB seed data and API payloads.
prompts.template:
Generate test data for [feature/field/module].
Output sets for:
- Valid / Happy path data
- Invalid data (wrong type, wrong format)
- Boundary values (min, max, min-1, max+1)
- Null / Empty / Whitespace
- Special characters and SQL injection strings
- Locale-specific data (if applicable: Unicode, RTL, emoji)
- Max length strings
Format: JSON (for API payloads) + SQL INSERT statements (for DB seeding).
Note data dependencies (foreign keys, required pre-conditions).

prompts.name: Regression Suite Prioritization
prompts.description: Categorize tests into Smoke, Sanity, and Full Regression based on release changes. Flags high-risk and flaky tests.
prompts.template:
Given [release notes / changed modules / sprint feature list], categorize test cases into:
- Smoke (5-10 critical path, max 10 min runtime): must-pass before any further testing
- Sanity (impacted area validation, 20-30 min): verify fixes/changes didn't break related flows
- Full Regression (complete coverage): scheduled or pre-release
Output: prioritized table with columns: TC-ID | Scenario | Suite | Justification | Risk Level
Flag: high-risk areas, cross-feature dependencies, known flaky tests.
Suggest parallel execution groupings for GitHub Actions.

prompts.name: GitHub Actions CI/CD Config
prompts.description: Generate GitHub Actions YAML to run Cypress or Playwright tests with parallelization, Mochawesome reports, and Linear/Slack notifications.
prompts.template:
Generate a GitHub Actions workflow YAML for [Cypress / Playwright] tests.
Include:
- Trigger: push to [main/develop], pull_request, and manual workflow_dispatch
- Node.js setup with caching
- Dependency install (npm ci)
- Environment variables from GitHub Secrets
- Test execution with shard/parallel support
- Mochawesome report generation and HTML artifact upload
- Screenshot/video artifact upload on failure
- Notification step: post result summary to [Slack webhook / Linear comment]
- Cache: node_modules and Cypress/Playwright browser binaries
Add inline comments explaining each step.

prompts.name: SQL Test Data Validation
prompts.description: Generate SQL queries to validate test data before/after test execution, supporting setup and teardown.
prompts.template:
Generate SQL queries for [feature/module] test data validation.
Include:
- Pre-condition check: verify required data exists before test run
- Post-condition check: verify DB state after test action (insert/update/delete)
- Cleanup/Teardown: delete test-generated records safely
- Count assertions: verify row counts match expected
- Constraint checks: FK integrity, NOT NULL, UNIQUE violations
Annotate each query with: purpose, table affected, when to run (before/after test).
Use parameterized style where applicable.

prompts.name: Mochawesome Report Config
prompts.description: Generate Mochawesome reporter setup for Cypress or Playwright with merge config for parallel runs.
prompts.template:
Generate Mochawesome reporter configuration for [Cypress / Playwright].
Include:
- Installation commands
- cypress.config.js or playwright.config.ts reporter setup
- mochawesome-merge config for combining parallel shard reports
- marge (mochawesome-report-generator) HTML generation command
- npm script shortcuts in package.json
- GitHub Actions step to upload HTML report as artifact
- Optional: add test retry count display in report
Output: config files + terminal commands + package.json scripts.

prompts.name: Sprint Test Summary
prompts.description: Generate a professional sprint-end test summary report for Agile/Scrum teams, suitable for sharing with PM/Dev leads.
prompts.template:
Generate a sprint test summary for [Sprint Name/Number].
Sections:
1. Sprint Overview: dates, team, scope
2. Test Execution Summary: Total | Passed | Failed | Skipped | Blocked (table)
3. Coverage: features tested, test types (Smoke/Regression/Exploratory)
4. Defects Found: severity breakdown table (Critical/Major/Minor)
5. Defects Fixed & Verified this Sprint
6. Automation Coverage Delta: new automated cases added
7. Risks & Blockers: anything affecting quality
8. Recommendations for Next Sprint
Tone: concise, professional, stakeholder-friendly. Use tables and bullet points.

prompts.name: Test Coverage Gap Analysis
prompts.description: Identify covered, missing, and risky test scenarios given a feature spec or user story.
prompts.template:
Analyze test coverage for [feature/user story].
Input: [existing test case IDs or descriptions]
Output:
1. Covered Scenarios: brief list with TC-IDs
2. Gaps Identified: missing test cases with description
3. Risk Areas: high-impact untested flows, critical paths
4. Recommended New Test Cases: tabular (TC-ID | Scenario | Type | Priority)
5. Automation Candidate Flag: which gaps should be automated first
Prioritize gaps: High / Medium / Low risk.

prompts.name: BDD Gherkin Scenario Writer
prompts.description: Convert manual test cases or user stories into Gherkin .feature file format. Ready for Cucumber integration.
prompts.template:
Convert [test cases / user story] into Gherkin .feature file format.
Rules:
- Feature: one per module
- Scenario / Scenario Outline: one per test case
- Use Background: for shared preconditions
- Use Scenario Outline + Examples table for data-driven tests
- Tags: @smoke, @regression, @sanity, @negative
- Steps: Given (precondition) | When (action) | Then (assertion) | And (additional step)
Output: ready-to-use .feature file. Add step definition stubs as comments below.

prompts.name: Performance Test Scaffold - k6
prompts.description: Generate a k6 load test script scaffold for API or web endpoints. For use when performance testing is needed.
prompts.template:
Generate a k6 load test script for [endpoint/feature].
Include:
- Import: http, check, sleep from k6
- Options: stages (ramp-up, sustained load, ramp-down), thresholds (p95 < Xms, error rate < Y%)
- Test scenarios: GET/POST with headers and auth token from environment
- Checks: status code, response time, response body field
- Teardown: cleanup if needed
- Output: k6 run command with env vars
Note: scaffold only — adjust VUs and thresholds to your SLA requirements.

prompts.name: Token Efficiency Guidelines
prompts.description: Internal guideline: keep all responses structured, compressed, and immediately actionable.
prompts.template:
Apply across all responses:
- Group repetitive steps into loops, functions, or tables
- Short, precise inline comments — no long explanations in code
- Summaries and tables first; detailed expandable sections second
- Always structured and actionable
- Flag: automation candidates, risk areas, n8n flow opportunities
- No filler text or redundant preamble

n8n_suggested_flows.action: Auto-create Linear bug ticket with test name, error message, screenshot URL, and environment
Pull GitHub Actions run results → format pass/fail summary → post to Slack or email

n8n_suggested_flows.trigger: New Linear issue created with label 'Ready for QA'
n8n_suggested_flows.action: Send story description to Claude API → generate draft test cases → post as Linear comment
Trigger GitHub Actions smoke test workflow → report result back as PR comment
Parse Mochawesome JSON → format summary (passed/failed/skipped) → post to QA Slack channel with report link
Pull Linear completed issues + test run stats → send to Claude API → generate sprint test summary → post to Confluence or Notion
Parse test results for retry patterns → flag tests that passed on retry → create Linear ticket tagged 'flaky-test'
